title: Emirates flight from Glasgow Airport to Dubai cancelled

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Glasgow Airport flight cancelled after U.S and Israel attack Iran (Image: Newsquest) Emirates flight EK028 to Dubai International has been cancelled after the General Civil Aviation Authority announced the temporary partial closure of the United Arab Emirates (UAE) airspace. The flight had been due to depart at 1.35pm and check-in had opened at the airport.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Passengers have been advised to contact Emirates for more information. A flight travelling from Dubai International landed shortly after 11am this morning. The airline has also cancelled a flight from Edinburgh Airport that had been due to depart at 8.55pm tonight. Emirates operates a daily service between Glasgow Airport and Dubai. No timeframe has been given for how long the airspace closure will be in place at this time.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The Glasgow Times previously reported the U.S has been massing its military in the Middle East since January, and on Saturday morning began major combat operations.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Glasgow Airport flight cancelled after U.S and Israel attack Iran
